name: Stuffed Pork Chops
recipeInstructions:
-------------------------------SEASONING MIX-------------------------------
1 tablespoon salt
1/2 teaspoon dry mustard
1 teaspoon onion powder
1/2 teaspoon rubbed sage
1 teaspoon ground cayenne pepper
1/2 teaspoon black pepper
1/2 teaspoon white pepper
1/2 teaspoon ground cumin
3/4 teaspoon garlic powder
Dried thyme leaves
---------------------------PORK CHOP INGREDIENTS---------------------------
6 pork chops, 1-3/4 inches thick
4 ounces diced green chilies (1 can)
1 cup pork or chicken stock
1 cup chopped onions
1/2 cup chopped green bell peppers
1/2 cup finely chopped green onions
2 teaspoons minced garlic
1. Process apples, 7 tablespoons butter, sugar, vanilla, and nutmeg in a food processor until smooth.
2. In a small bowl, combine seasoning mix ingredients.
3. Prepare pork chops by cutting a pocket into each to hold stuffing.
4. In a large skillet, brown ground pork over high heat; add onions, bell peppers, garlic, and 2 tablespoons seasoning mix, stirring well for about 5 minutes.
5. Stir in green chilies and their juice, continuing to cook until well browned (6-8 minutes).
6. Add stock and cook 5 minutes, stirring frequently.
7. Stir in bread crumbs and cook an additional 2 minutes.
8. Sprinkle remaining seasoning mix on chops and pockets; press into meat.
9. Arrange chops with pocket up in a baking pan; stuff each with about 1/4 cup stuffing.
10. Reserve remaining stuffing for last 20 minutes of baking.
11. Bake at 400°F until pork is done (about 1 hour 10 minutes).
12. Serve immediately, arranging chops on top of portions of stuffing.
